questions.question: What is the primary purpose of reading a dictionary in UNIX system programming?
options: To find words matching a pattern
questions.explanation: To find words matching a pattern

questions.question: Which UNIX command is used to read a dictionary file and perform word matching?
What is the output of the command `grep -f dictionary.txt file.txt`?
options: Lines in file.txt that do not match any word in dictionary.txt
Lines in file.txt that match any word in dictionary.txt
questions.explanation: Lines in file.txt that match any word in dictionary.txt

questions.question: What is the difference between `grep -v` and `grep`?
options: `grep -v` finds lines that do not match any word in the dictionary
`grep` finds lines that match any word in the dictionary
`grep -v` finds lines that match any word in the dictionary
Both `grep` and `grep -v` find lines that match any word in the dictionary
questions.explanation: `grep -v` finds lines that do not match any word in the dictionary

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `-f` option in the `grep` command?
options: To find lines containing a specific word
